---
title: "Program Learning Outcomes"
subtitle: "What a Freshwater Science graduate is expected to be able to do"
date: last-modified
---

The current authority for **Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs)** is the
**v2 PLO set** finalised on **28 April 2026** by Dr. Nelson Jatel, PAg,
Lecturer and Program Advisor (memo: `docs/reference/learning-outcomes/UBCO_Memo_PLOs_v2_20260428.docx`).
The v2 set consolidated the v1 draft (14 outcomes) down to **8 outcomes
in 4 categories**, based on:

- Student focus-group feedback (Freshwater Science majors),
- Faculty working-session input, and
- Best-practice benchmarking against Penn State, Washington State,
  CSUSB, SUNY, U Michigan-Dearborn, and comparator BSc Freshwater
  programs at UW-Milwaukee, Western Michigan, and UW-Madison.

The 5-to-7-outcome range is the sector norm; v1's 14 was flagged by
both faculty and benchmarks as too long for meaningful assessment.

# The eight v2 outcomes

## Depth and Breadth of Knowledge

1. Explain water processes in the context of planetary cycles, surface
   processes, and environmental systems, including human alterations to
   freshwater ecosystems.
2. Describe the physical, chemical, and biological structure and
   function of freshwater systems.

## Application of Knowledge

3. Collect, organise, and analyse water and environmental science data
   using appropriate field, laboratory, computational (e.g., R, Python),
   and statistical techniques, and represent results in appropriate
   graphical formats.
4. Apply the scientific method and quantitative reasoning to develop
   conceptual models, test hypotheses, and evaluate trade-offs in
   freshwater management decisions.
5. Acquire, critically assess, and synthesise freshwater science
   literature, Indigenous knowledge systems (ways of knowing), data
   sources, and spatial resources (e.g., maps, remotely sensed data) to
   inform interpretation and decision-making.
6. Develop water-related policy options and communicate them
   effectively to advance uptake by decision-makers.

## Communication

7. Communicate limnology and environmental concepts to diverse
   audiences through formal written reports, oral presentations,
   technical posters, illustrations, and clear field, laboratory, and
   code documentation.

## Professional Conduct

8. Demonstrate professional ethics, safe field and laboratory practice,
   effective teamwork, and respectful engagement with Indigenous
   Peoples, lands, and rights-holders.

# How each course contributes — the I / R / M methodology

The curriculum-map methodology used to trace these PLOs through the
program uses three coding labels for each course-learning-outcome
(CLO) → PLO link:

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **I** | Introductory — the course introduces the concept; students encounter it for the first time. |
| **R** | Reinforcement — the course practises the concept; students extend prior exposure. |
| **M** | Mastery — the course expects students to demonstrate the concept independently. |

Combined with an **X** marker showing which CLO maps to which PLO,
this is the same framework UBC's Faculty of Science uses for the
EECB program (Ecology, Evolution, and Conservation Biology) and other
B.Sc. programs.

::: {.callout-note}
## Pilot: EESC 301 Limnology
A worked example of the methodology for **EESC 301 (Limnology)**
mapped to the EECB program PLOs is committed at
`docs/reference/learning-outcomes/EESC 301 - Mapping to EECB Program Learning Outcomes - NJ.xlsx`.
It captures six course learning outcomes, links each to one or more
program PLOs, and codes the depth (I / R / M). This is the template
that needs to be replicated for every Freshwater Science required and
elective course.
:::

# Curriculum map — pending build

Following the v2 PLO finalisation memo (§7 Recommended Next Steps), the
next deliverable is the **full curriculum map**: every required
Freshwater Science course mapped against the 8 PLOs with I / R / M
coding. This page will eventually render that matrix as a sortable
table:

::: {.callout-warning}
## Scaffold placeholder
The curriculum-map matrix (rows = course, columns = PLO 1-8, cells = I / R / M)
is not yet built. The schema for `outcomes/data/curriculum_map.yml`
plus a script to render it as the table above is queued. Each course's
mapping requires a structured conversation with the instructor, using
the EESC 301 pilot as the template.
:::

# Version history

| Version | Date | Outcomes | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **v2** (current) | 28 April 2026 | 8 in 4 categories | Refined by Dr. Nelson Jatel, PAg, following student + faculty consultation and best-practice benchmarking. Memo: `UBCO_Memo_PLOs_v2_20260428.docx`. |
| v1 (draft) | 2025 | 14 in 5 categories | Superseded by v2. Mapping of v1 → v2 in the memo §6 crosswalk. Draft: `BSc Freshwater Science Draft PLOs (V1).docx`. |

# Assessment plan — pending

The v2 finalisation memo flags two further deliverables:

1. **Direct assessment artifacts** in EESC 301, EESC 402, and the
   capstone course, identifying which artifact assesses which PLO.
2. **Three-year review cycle** aligned with UBC institutional
   assessment guidance.

Both are pending and will be tracked in the project's GitHub issues.

# References

- UBCO Freshwater Science Program. (2026). *Finalisation of Freshwater
  Science Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs), v2.* Internal memo, 28 April 2026.
  (`docs/reference/learning-outcomes/UBCO_Memo_PLOs_v2_20260428.docx`)
- UBCO Freshwater Science Program. (2025). *Draft Program Learning
  Outcomes (V1).* Internal document.
  (`docs/reference/learning-outcomes/BSc Freshwater Science Draft PLOs (V1).docx`)
- UBC Faculty of Science EECB program curriculum-map template (used as
  the basis for the I / R / M methodology and the EESC 301 worked
  example).
- Penn State Office of Planning, Assessment and Institutional Research.
  [Program Learning Objectives](https://opair.psu.edu/assessment/loa-handbook/program-learning-objectives/).
- Washington State University Office of Assessment for Curricular
  Effectiveness. (2024). [Quick Guide to Program-level Student
  Learning Outcomes](https://ace.wsu.edu/documents/2015/02/quick-guide-to-program-slos.pdf/).
- University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee. [Freshwater Sciences, BS — Program
  Learning Outcomes](https://catalog.uwm.edu/freshwater-sciences/freshwater-sciences-bs/).
